pattern: Because all values are positive, growing a window only raises its sum and shrinking only lowers it, so a two-pointer window can expand to reach the target then contract from the left to find the shortest qualifying span in one linear pass.
hints: All numbers are positive, which means extending a subarray can never decrease its sum and removing a leading element can never increase it.
Keep a running window sum; widen it by advancing the right end and, whenever the sum is at least the target, try to narrow it from the left.

Each time the window sum reaches the target, record the window length and then subtract the left element and advance the left pointer, repeating while the sum still qualifies; return the smallest length seen, or 0 if none ever reached the target.

edge_inputs.description: shortest window is a trailing pair
a single element already meets the target
total sum is below the target so no subarray qualifies
entire array needed to reach the target
single element exactly equal to target
single element just below target yields nothing
large early value makes a one-element answer

stress.description: large positive array with a target reachable by a short span
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 10000) for _ in range(n)]
    target = rng.randint(1, sum(nums))
    return [target, nums]
stress.note: target capped at the total sum so an answer usually exists, exercising the window contraction
